title: Delicate Yeasty Moroccan Lamb Harira
description: A light and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring lamb, Thai green curry paste, and a yeasty twist, all in a hearty harira form, made Whole30-friendly by using compliant ingredients and cooking methods.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the Thai green curry paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to combine with the lamb and onion mixture.
Add the chicken broth, tomato paste, celery, parsley, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 hour.
Stir in the active dry yeast and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the yeast is fully dissolved and the harira has thickened slightly.
Serve the harira hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
